name: Theory of Constraints (TOC)
what_it_is: Every system has exactly one constraint limiting throughput - identify and exploit it before optimizing anything else.
core_mechanic: Identify the constraint. Exploit it (max value without additional investment). Subordinate everything else to it. Elevate (invest to remove it). Repeat - once removed, a new constraint appears.
when_to_use: Optimizations aren't producing results / Multiple things feel like problems but can't find focus / Execution is slow and cause isn't obvious / Team capacity is stretched
when_not_to_use: Strategy formation - TOC optimizes existing systems / The constraint is genuinely unclear and you lack data
ideaos_application: Agent 3 identifies the most likely constraint, maps what is being sub-optimized by focusing on non-constraints, produces a "stop doing this" list alongside the constraint exploitation recommendation.
